repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 has only Manski bounded-outcome wrappers, not policy-score sensitivity support functions

published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020 partial-R2 sensitivity; ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 MSM IPW sensitivity; AdjahoChristensen2025 external-validity policy robustness

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 studies joint misspecification value intervals, not CH/MSM/shift scalar-calibration frontiers

published_prior_art.cite: DiegertMastenPoirier2025 compares sensitivity parameters axiomatically, but not finite policy-score rank-one calibration across CH/MSM/shift
HuangPimentel2025 contrasts variance-based weighting sensitivity with marginal Linfinity sensitivity, but not an open-set two-policy policy-regret witness

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter emitted no literature checklist and §5 misses Diegert-Masten-Poirier 2025 and Masten-Poirier-Ren 2025, the closest sensitivity-parameter comparison/relaxation papers; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the frontier is sharpened against this literature.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The open-set penalty separation is framed as an internal warning and does not name a published workflow that actually equates CH/variance penalties with MSM or covariate-shift policy penalties.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit literature checklist entry and §5 comparison for Diegert-Masten-Poirier 2025 and Masten-Poirier-Ren 2025, explaining why their sensitivity-parameter comparison frameworks do not subsume the policy-score frontier.

To meet the flagship floor, recast Conjecture 1 as a strict equivalence/non-equivalence frontier between two named published policy criteria with a new observable threshold, not just equality of three support-function norm columns.
Either name a published CH-to-MSM or R2-to-policy-ranking workflow targeted by Conjecture 2, or demote Conjecture 2 to a supporting lemma rather than a novelty-bearing negative result.

Make observability of W_pi explicit by giving the formula or primitive conditions under which the finite diagnostic Q_{Pi_*} is computable from P for the Jin-Xu-Zheng-Zhou and Adjaho-Christensen criteria.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison and flagship claim: either lift Conjecture 1 to a named-criteria frontier over the missed comparison literature or lower the target tier.
